syntaxReference:
Helper methods (decomposition): 
private void moveToWall() { while (frontIsClear()) { move(); } }

Implementing turnRight in base Karel: 
private void turnRight() { turnLeft(); turnLeft(); turnLeft(); }
commonPatterns:
Move to wall safely: 
while (frontIsClear()) { move(); } // stop when frontIsBlocked()

Conditional action each step: 
while (frontIsClear()) { if (beepersPresent()) { pickBeeper(); } move(); }

Stair/hurdle climb (SuperKarel): 
turnLeft(); move(); turnRight(); move(); turnRight(); move(); turnLeft();

Check-last-corner cleanup: 
// For actions that must include the last corner, do the action after each move OR place before entering loop as needed.
quickTips: Guard moves with frontIsClear() to avoid crashes.
Write small helpers for repeated sequences (e.g., turnRight, moveToWall).
State pre/postconditions for every helper and preserve them during refactors.
